_about: The Morning Queue — one desk shift for the Adventuring Guild Teller (AGT musing). Sixteen visitors, in queue order. Every `check` resolves against an entry in references.json, so the desk is actually verifiable, not just narrated. Each visitor also carries an `inspections` block — the readings the Glass (examine) and Scale (weigh) tools return for THIS visitor's item; see ../INSPECTION-TOOLS.md for the tool + limit design. Data is prototype canon (2026-07-13); correct by `id`. Enemies are wild-magic apparitions / mana beasts by design — nothing that depopulates. See ../MORNING-QUEUE.md for the schema contract.

schema.inspection.shape: inspections { glass { reading, relevant }, scale { reading, amount, unit, relevant } } — reading is required content prose; amount/unit/relevant are authored metadata; `relevant` is design-only and NEVER shown to the player (see ../INSPECTION-TOOLS.md §2-3).

visitors.name: Wrenna Sixpence

claim.summary: A jar of "moonwort" delivered against the apothecary's standing order.

glass.reading: Silver underside, five lobes, cold to the touch.

visitors.player_story: Flip the book to Moonwort — silver underside, five lobes, cold to the touch. Hold the leaf to the lamp: silver, five lobes. Weigh it — 3 drams, and the order takes 2 to 4. Stamp APPROVED.
visitors.notes: The easy opener that teaches the loop: one Glass-vs-Book identity check, one Scale-vs-limit amount check, clean yes. The one shipped visitor where the amount is a real (passing) check.

visitors.name: Hulbr Odd-Eye

claim.summary: Rank Bronze, taking the "Cistern wisp-swarm" posting (Bronze or better).

glass.reading: A bronze guild card, seal pressed clean; the corners worn smooth from long handling; the dues column freshly inked and dated this quarter.

visitors.player_story: Pull his card from the rank ledger — Bronze, dues current. Posting slip says Bronze-or-better. Ranks clear, posting open. Stamp ASSIGNED.
visitors.notes: Second clean yes, different check type — teaches the rank ledger before any reject. Both tools are decoys: the card looks bronze, but only the ledger is authoritative.

visitors.name: Pell Marrow

claim.summary: A pressed "kingsfoil" sprig for the infirmary's standing order.

failure.reason: It is common yarrow. Same flat white head, but kingsfoil's stem is square and yarrow's is round — the book calls the square stem the only sure tell.

glass.reading: Round stem, feathered leaf, flat white head.

scale.reading: One sprig on the pan — a single stem.

visitors.player_story: Book to Kingsfoil — square stem. Roll the stem: round. Cross-check yarrow — match. Stamp REJECTED, note "yarrow, not kingsfoil."
visitors.notes: First reject. The tell is in the reference, not memory — rewards actually looking it up. Amount decoy: the sprig meets the order's total, but the plant is wrong — Scale reassures, Glass condemns.

visitors.name: Doss Yellowknife

claim.summary: Rank Silver, wants the "Barrow gloam" posting (Gold or better).

failure.reason: Under-ranked. Silver card, Gold gate — one rung short.

glass.reading: A silver guild card, edges still bright and the board stiff with newness; the issue stamp dated this season — barely a month out of the registry.

visitors.player_story: Ledger card reads Silver. Posting reads Gold-or-better. One rung short. Stamp REFUSED, hand back the slip.
visitors.notes: The rank gate as a hard line — the desk doesn't bend it; that's Floor work. Both tools decoy: the card is genuinely silver; the gate, not the card, is the fail.

visitors.name: Ivy Threnody

claim.summary: A completion token for "Recover the ledger of Ashford Mill" — brings a ledger, asks the bounty be paid to her.

failure.reason: The token is real; the quest was assigned to a different party (Odd-Eye's, still afield). She found the ledger loose on the road — right proof, wrong claimant.

checks.result: mismatch: payee is not the assigned party

glass.reading: The archive wax is whole, the impression deep and clean — the true guild seal, unbroken and struck from the original archive die.

scale.reading: The token disc: 4 drams of good brass.

visitors.player_story: Match the token's seal — genuine. Pull the quest slip: assigned, party out, not returned. Payee doesn't match. Stamp HELD, "return to assigned party."
visitors.notes: Real proof, wrong claimant — a second axis (who) beyond the usual what. Glass decoy: it truthfully confirms the seal is genuine, and the answer is still reject — the fail is on the posting, not the token. Under STRICT_BINARY this hold judges as reject (its binary field).

visitors.name: Bartholomew Quill

claim.summary: A "griffon primary feather" as proof for the "Cliff-nest survey" bounty.

failure.reason: Forgery. A goose quill dyed with saffron; a true primary is twin-barbed and won't hold dye evenly, and its rachis is hollow.

glass.reading: Single-barbed; the dye lifts under a wet cloth; the rachis is solid, not hollow.

visitors.player_story: Book to Griffon Feather — twin-barbed, dye-fast, hollow rachis. Wet cloth down the vane: color lifts. Bend the rachis: solid. Two tells fail. Stamp REJECTED, flag "dyed goose quill."
visitors.notes: First deliberate deception — the item is real-looking but engineered to pass a lazy glance. Scale decoy: proof-of-survey has no standing order, so the weight measures against nothing — the Glass carries the whole check.

visitors.name: Sister Coll

claim.summary: Files a quest: "Cleanse the well-shrine of the drowned saint" — a water-bound apparition; requires a cleric- or water-warded taker.

failure.reason: No cleric- or water-warded party is registered as active in the guild at all. Not "none in today" — no one on the roster could ever take it.

glass.reading: A filing slip, not a specimen — only ink under the lens.

scale.reading: Paper weighs nothing worth a reading.

visitors.player_story: Item n/a, rank n/a — fieldability. Scan the roster's ward column: no active party carries a cleric or water ward. No eligible taker on the books, whenever they come or go. Stamp UNFIELDABLE, "no warded party registered."
visitors.notes: The third check as a roster-capability question, not a scheduling one. What you enroll (see Vane) changes this. Both tools decoy: a spoken filing has nothing to examine or weigh — the check is entirely on the roster.

visitors.name: Ganton Reeve

claim.summary: Rank Gold, but his ledger card is missing — asks you to take his word for the "Wyrm-scale collection" posting (Gold).

failure.reason: No card, no dues record; word alone isn't proof at the desk.

checks.result: no card under Reeve or the alias offered

glass.reading: He lays no card down — nothing to examine but his word.

visitors.player_story: Search the ledger — no card under Reeve, or the alias. Nothing to check against. Stamp WITHHELD, "present rank card."
visitors.notes: Absence of proof is its own reject — the desk verifies, it doesn't trust. The tools return empty readings, which is itself the tell: there is no item to inspect.

visitors.name: Odile Vantry

claim.summary: A vial of "quicksilver tears" (mercuric) against a warded order.

failure.reason: The substance is correct; the order requires a hazard seal she hasn't paid for — right goods, incomplete request.

checks.result: amount within range; no hazard seal

glass.reading: It beads, it splits, it shows silver — true quicksilver.

visitors.player_story: Book confirms the vial — beads, splits, silver. Substance right. Weigh it: 2 drams, within the order. But the order reads "hazard-sealed transport": no seal. Stamp CONDITIONAL, "correct goods, obtain hazard seal."
visitors.notes: Right item, right amount, incomplete request — a softer half-fail that pressures the strictly-binary desk (AGT.5). Both tools pass and it is STILL a reject: the missing hazard seal lives only on the posting, not on any tool. Under STRICT_BINARY this conditional judges as reject (its binary field).

visitors.name: Two-Rivers

claim.summary: A rank-up application: Copper to Bronze, with a logbook of three completed Copper postings.

glass.reading: Three slips, three distinct seals — each grain its own.

visitors.player_story: Not a quest — a rank-up (AGT.4's second desk job). Count sealed entries: three. Cross-check each seal against archived slips: all match, all his. Stamp PROMOTED, issue Bronze card.
visitors.notes: A satisfying yes after a run of nos; introduces the archive cross-check the next visitor subverts. The Glass reads the seals as distinct — the identity tool works on paperwork too, checked against the Archive.

visitors.name: Marn Culpepper

claim.summary: A rank-up logbook, Copper to Bronze — three postings, one seal reused twice.

failure.reason: Forged padding. Two of the three entries carry the same seal impression from one posting; only two distinct completions exist — threshold not met.

glass.reading: Three slips — but two seals share one grain, struck from a single die.

visitors.player_story: Count entries: three. But two seals are identical to the grain — same posting logged twice. Archive shows one slip for that seal. Threshold not met. Stamp DENIED, flag "duplicated seal."
visitors.notes: The subtle forgery — Quill's trick applied to paperwork; rewards a careful eye on the seals. The Glass is the load-bearing tool: it reveals the duplicated grain the Archive then confirms.

visitors.name: The Widow Ashe

claim.summary: Files a quest: "Retrieve my late husband's blade from the Sunken Barrow" — the barrow where Odd-Eye's party is still afield.

truth.flag_floor: Retrieval match — a party (Odd-Eye's) is already in that barrow; there may be a blade waiting to be matched to a returning party. This is the gear-left-behind seed the pitch names.

glass.reading: A spoken petition — nothing under the lens.

visitors.player_story: Item n/a; rank n/a; fieldability — yes, a party's already in that barrow. Stamp FILED, and tag it for the Floor as a retrieval match.
visitors.notes: The closer: a clean desk-stamp that hands a thread to Pillar II — the seam between the two chairs. Tools decoy: a filing has nothing to inspect; the roster carries it.

visitors.name: Perrick Vane

claim.summary: Presents a transfer card from the Hollowmere chapter; asks to be entered on the active roster as Silver.

glass.reading: A triple-notched wave pressed in wax; dues stamped and dated.

scale.reading: Transfer card: 3 drams of waxed board.

visitors.player_story: New task type — welcome. Book to the chapter-cipher table: Hollowmere's mark is a triple-notched wave; the seal matches to the notch. Dues stamped and dated. Copy his name, rank, and wards into the roster book; stamp ENROLLED.
visitors.notes: The roster is a thing you WRITE to. Enrolling Vane (a water ward) is what would make Sister Coll's shrine fieldable next time — the mechanics interlock. The Glass examines the card's seal, checked against the Chapter Ciphers — the same examine loop as an herb.

visitors.name: "Sable" (card: Cassius Lorn)

claim.summary: A transfer card from the Ironquill chapter; asks to join as Gold.

failure.reason: Forged card. Ironquill's cipher is a five-bar comb; this seal has four bars, and the dues date is stamped in ink — Ironquill seals in wax, not ink. An impostor claiming a rank he never held.

checks.result: mismatch: four bars, ink not wax

glass.reading: Four bars on the comb-mark, and the dues line is ink — not pressed wax.

visitors.player_story: Cipher table to Ironquill: five bars, wax seal. Count the bars: four. The dues mark is ink, not pressed wax. Two tells fail. Do NOT write to the roster; stamp REJECTED, flag "forged Ironquill card."
visitors.notes: Welcome's failure mode — the forgery check now guards who gets ADDED, not just who gets paid. The Glass reveals four bars / ink; the Chapter Ciphers say five bars / wax — a Glass-vs-Rulebook mismatch, exactly like the yarrow tell.

visitors.name: Goodwife Tamsin

claim.summary: Commissions the guild to fetch "phoenix-ember resin" and asks what it will cost.

checks.result: match: a Gold party reaches Floor 5

glass.reading: A commission slip — the resin itself is still down in the deep.

scale.reading: Nothing on the pan; this is a request, not a delivery.

visitors.player_story: Fifth task type — dungeon-drop request, a small pipeline: (1) real drop? yes. (2) in season? summer, yes. (3) floor? Floor 4. (4) can we reach it? a party rated to Floor 5. Then the payout: base 40g x Floor-4 depth multiplier (2.0) + in-season premium 10g = 90g. Stamp QUOTED, log the commission.
visitors.notes: The multi-gate calc loop — first task with arithmetic. Heavier than a binary stamp; may belong to a later shift rank. Tools decoy: there is no item in hand to examine or weigh — a commission is measured by the Drop / Season / Roster / Payout tables.

visitors.name: Ostler Bram

claim.summary: Commissions "frost-lily petal" for a quench-bath and expects a quote.

failure.reason: Real drop, but out of season — frost-lily only forms when the low floors ice over in deep winter; it's summer. (Second fail: it's on Floor 7 and the deepest active party reaches Floor 6 — the reach gate fails too.)

checks.result: out of season — short-circuits the quote

glass.reading: A commission slip — the petal itself is still afield.

scale.reading: Nothing on the pan; a request, not a delivery.

visitors.player_story: Same pipeline: (1) real drop? yes. (2) in season? winter-only, and it's summer — gate fails here. (Further: Floor 7, deepest party reaches Floor 6 — a second fail.) No payout possible. Stamp DECLINED, "out of season" (+ "beyond current reach").
visitors.notes: The drop pipeline short-circuits — one failed gate kills the quote before you even reach the arithmetic. Tools decoy: nothing in hand; the Season Wheel and Roster reach carry the reject.

visitors.name: Nessa Broom

claim.summary: A jar of "moonwort" against the apothecary's standing order — a generous harvest.

failure.reason: Correct plant, but the apothecary's order accepts only 2 to 4 drams per delivery; this jar holds 6. The cap exists to prevent oversupply — excess moonwort spoils before it can be dispensed.

checks.result: over range — 6 drams exceeds the 4-dram cap

glass.reading: Silver underside, five lobes, cold to the touch — moonwort, certain.

visitors.player_story: Book to Moonwort — silver underside, five lobes. Correct plant. Weigh it: 6 drams, and the order takes only 2 to 4. Over the cap. Stamp REJECTED, note "over the per-delivery limit."
visitors.notes: The amount-fail complement to wren-sixpence (#1): same herb, same order, wrong weight. Glass confirms identity and decoys — it reassures with a perfect match; the Scale condemns. Teaches that right plant + wrong amount = reject, and that the Scale has teeth even when the Glass passes.
